examples: Today the Euromarket has become an indispensable element of the international financial market.
That was followed by what one economist called the "Bigger Bang": the fast growth in offshore financial transactions funnelled through London thanks to the Euromarket.
Thee Euromarket, which is defined as a market where financial instruments traded are denominated in currencies other than the currency of the country where the market is located.
London's status as a global financial centre is, in part, an historical accident driven by American regulations in the 1960s that created an offshore lending business (the Euromarket) in Britain.
